keys.description: The compact ResolvedPermissionSet per principal. THE hot path — read on every gated request. principal_type is one of `User` / `PlatformUser` / `ServiceAccount`. application_id + tenant_id are load-bearing (same Identity's User rows on different Applications resolve to different sets).
Full role catalogue per (tenant, application). Read on the tenant plane's `GET /rbac/roles` — sub-100ms P99. Includes system roles seeded for this tenant + custom roles the tenant created.

Full permission catalogue per Application. Populated at boot by PermissionCatalogueLoader from every module's permissions.json. Long TTL because permission names are ~static across deploys; invalidated on deploy via the release pipeline calling `php artisan rbac:sync-role-definitions --refresh-catalogue`.

role_definitions rows for an Application (business_type_slugs, i18n labels, min_tier, default_permissions[]). Read by the TenantDefaultRoleSeeder on TenantProvisioned. Long TTL since metadata rarely changes.

The role slugs held by a principal (short list, distinct from the full permission set). Used by the tenant-plane `GET /rbac/principals/{principal}/roles` and by delegation resolvers to walk the role chain.

A tenant-scoped marker used by the cross-node broadcast pattern. When RBAC mutates on Node A, the invalidation event is broadcast on `tenant.{id}.rbac.principal.{principal_id}.permissions` (Redis pubsub). Node B receives, sets `rbac:cache:stale:{tenant_id}` for 60 seconds, and next request re-resolves. Bounds the eventual-consistency window without requiring active cache pruning.

60s debounce guard preventing repeated permission checks for the same (principal, permission) tuple from stampeding the resolver on cache miss. Set by `PermissionResolver::resolve()` on first miss; subsequent misses within the window fall to DB directly without stampede coordination.
